meals.name: Sweet Potato & Black Bean Hash
meals.desc: Sauteed onions, bell peppers, topped with sliced avocado
ingredients: 2 medium sweet potatoes, peeled and diced small
1 can (15 oz) black beans, drained and rinsed
steps: Heat coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add diced sweet potatoes and cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender and lightly browned.
Add onion and bell peppers. Cook 4-5 minutes until softened.
Stir in black beans, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Divide between two plates and top each with sliced avocado.

meals.name: Herb-Crusted Chicken Thighs
ingredients: 1 large head broccoli, cut into florets
steps: Preheat oven to 425F. Rinse quinoa and combine with water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce to low, cover, and cook 15 minutes.
Mix olive oil, garlic, thyme, rosemary, salt, and pepper. Rub over chicken thighs.
Place chicken skin-side up on a baking sheet. Roast for 20 minutes.
Toss broccoli florets with a drizzle of olive oil, salt, and pepper. Add to the baking sheet around the chicken.
Roast another 15-20 minutes until chicken reaches 165F and broccoli is crispy at the edges.
Fluff quinoa with a fork and serve alongside the chicken and broccoli.

meals.name: Celery & Almond Butter
meals.desc: With a sprinkle of hemp seeds
steps: Spread almond butter into the celery grooves or serve on the side for dipping.
Sprinkle hemp seeds over the top.

meals.name: Chia Pudding
meals.desc: Coconut milk, sliced strawberries, and unsweetened coconut flakes
steps: Whisk chia seeds, coconut milk, honey, and vanilla in a bowl or jar.
Stir well, making sure there are no clumps.
Cover and refrigerate overnight (or at least 4 hours) until thick and pudding-like.
In the morning, stir and divide into two bowls. Top with sliced strawberries and coconut flakes.

meals.name: Shrimp Tacos
meals.desc: Corn tortillas, cabbage slaw, avocado, and lime crema (cashew cream)
ingredients: 1 lb wild-caught shrimp, peeled and deveined
3 limes (1 juiced for crema, 2 for serving)
steps: Toss shrimp with chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat coconut oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook shrimp 2-3 minutes per side until pink and opaque. Set aside.
Make the lime crema by mixing cashew cream with the juice of 1 lime and a pinch of salt.
Warm corn tortillas in a dry skillet or directly over a gas flame for 15-20 seconds per side.
Build tacos: layer shredded cabbage, shrimp, avocado slices, and a drizzle of lime crema.
Garnish with cilantro and serve with lime wedges.

meals.name: Fresh Berries & Pumpkin Seeds
meals.desc: Blueberries and raspberries with pumpkin seeds
steps: Rinse berries and divide between two bowls.
Top each with pumpkin seeds.

meals.name: Banana Oat Pancakes
meals.desc: Mashed banana, oats, egg, cinnamon, cooked in coconut oil with fresh berries
steps: Blend or mash bananas, oats, eggs, and cinnamon into a batter. Let it sit for 5 minutes so the oats soften.
Heat coconut oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Pour about 3 tablespoons of batter per pancake. Cook 2-3 minutes until bubbles form on the surface.
Flip and cook another 1-2 minutes until golden.
Repeat with remaining batter (makes about 6-8 small pancakes).
Serve topped with fresh berries and a drizzle of honey if desired.

meals.name: Mediterranean Chickpea Salad
meals.desc: Cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, olives, parsley, and lemon-olive oil dressing
ingredients: 1 can (15 oz) chickpeas, drained and rinsed
steps: Combine chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, olives, and parsley in a bowl.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper together.
Pour dressing over the salad and toss to combine.
Serve immediately or refrigerate for 30 minutes to let flavors meld.

meals.name: Baked Salmon
meals.desc: Ginger-tamari glaze, steamed broccoli, and jasmine rice
ingredients: 1 large head broccoli, cut into florets
steps: Start the rice: rinse jasmine rice, combine with water in a pot, bring to a boil, reduce to low, cover, and cook 15 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400F. Mix tamari, grated ginger, garlic, sesame oil, and honey to make the glaze.
Place salmon on a lined baking sheet. Spoon glaze over each fillet.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until salmon flakes easily.
While salmon bakes, steam broccoli florets for 4-5 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Fluff rice and serve salmon over rice with broccoli on the side. Spoon any remaining glaze from the pan over the fish.

meals.name: Sliced Pear & Walnuts
meals.desc: Fresh pear with a handful of walnuts
steps: Slice pears and divide between two plates.
Add a small handful of walnuts to each.

meals.name: Scrambled Eggs
meals.desc: Sauteed spinach, mushrooms, and cherry tomatoes with sprouted grain toast
steps: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add mushrooms and cook 4-5 minutes until golden.
Add cherry tomatoes and cook 2 minutes until they start to soften.
Add spinach and stir until just wilted, about 1 minute.
Push vegetables to one side. Crack eggs into the pan and scramble gently, folding in the vegetables as they cook.
Season with salt and pepper.
Toast the bread and serve alongside the eggs.

meals.name: Herb-Roasted Chicken Quarters
meals.desc: Roasted root vegetables (carrots, parsnips, sweet potato) and green salad
ingredients: 3 carrots, peeled and cut into chunks
3 parsnips, peeled and cut into chunks
steps: Preheat oven to 425F. Toss carrots, parsnips, and sweet potato with 2 tbsp olive oil, garlic, salt, and pepper. Spread in a roasting pan.
Rub chicken quarters with remaining olive oil, thyme, rosemary, salt, and pepper. Nestle them on top of the vegetables.
Squeeze lemon halves over everything and tuck the spent halves into the pan.
Roast for 45-50 minutes until chicken skin is golden and crispy and internal temp reaches 165F.
Let rest 5 minutes before serving.
Toss mixed greens with a simple squeeze of lemon and drizzle of olive oil. Serve alongside the chicken and roasted vegetables.

meals.name: Trail Mix
meals.desc: Almonds, walnuts, dried cranberries, and dairy-free dark chocolate chips
steps: Combine all ingredients in a bowl and toss.
Divide into two portions. Store extras in an airtight container.

Pantry & Condiments: Cashew cream or raw cashews (small bag)
